steps.title: Keep the proposed sequence
steps.content: The student needs to recount the researchers' proposed sequence of events. The notes give a clear order: Chrysalis likely came very close to Saturn around 160 million years ago, tidal forces broke the moon apart, and the resulting debris formed Saturn's rings.

steps.title: Remove reversed or partial accounts
steps.content: Choice A reverses the cause and effect by saying debris from the rings caused Chrysalis to break apart. Choice B mentions a moon breaking apart but omits the timing and the rings. Choice D gives only the general tidal-force principle rather than the proposed event sequence. The answer is C.
